title: Resource APIs, Idempotency, Concurrency, Pagination, and Consistency · Changed-Schema Lab
summary: An independent service team receives a different tenant, traffic, trust, persistence, and failure profile and transfers resource apis, idempotency, concurrency, pagination, and consistency into a new consistent resource API and operation ledger. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of consistent resource API and operation ledger with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and ev…
objectives: Model resource states, legal transitions, commands, representations, invariants, tombstones, and conflict outcomes independently of table layout.
Scope idempotency keys to principal and operation, compare canonical request fingerprints, store outcome atomically, expire deliberately, and reject mismatches.
Connect version columns, ETags, preconditions, atomic updates, affected-row checks, conflict disclosure, and safe retry behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Lab Resource State Machine
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab team handles case 1911 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to join idempotency fingerprints, optimistic concurrency, and transactional outbox evidence; reject a replay before any external effect; then change the method and preserve both status and durable-effect evidence. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Lab Resource State Machine.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for model resource states, legal transitions, commands, representations, invariants, tombstones, and conflict outcomes independently of table layout. For Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Lab Resource State Machine, keep Resource State Machine connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

lines.text: Publishing an event immediately after COMMIT makes state and message delivery atomic.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Persist state and an outbox fact atomically, publish with deduplication and ordering assumptions, and reconcile stuck or duplicate delivery.

options.text: For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Outbox Consistency happy path, omit method, authority, credential, byte, time, redirect, retry, and cleanup boundaries, then accept plausible output. This does not satisfy Outbox Consistency for consistent resource API and operation ledger.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Outbox Consistency condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

Keep the plausible Outbox Consistency output and hide the violated consistent resource API and operation ledger assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Outbox Consistency.

steps.title: Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Lab Outbox Consistency
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Outbox Consistency: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A lab team handles case 4958 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to join idempotency fingerprints, optimistic concurrency, and transactional outbox evidence; reject a replay before any external effect; then make OpenAPI and runtime disagree on nullability and block the release. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Persist state and an outbox fact atomically, publish with deduplication and ordering assumptions, and reconcile stuck or duplicate delivery.

Evidence target: Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Lab Outbox Consistency.
steps.why: Retrieving Outbox Consistency through the concrete consistent resource API and operation ledger case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Lab Outbox Consistency, keep Outbox Consistency connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Persist state and an outbox fact atomically, publish with deduplication and ordering assumptions, and reconcile stuck or duplicate delivery.

hints: For Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Lab Outbox Consistency, name the method, authority, body, byte, time, and credential boundary, Outbox Consistency precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Lab Outbox Consistency, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A lab team handles case 4958 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to join idempotency fingerprints, optimistic concurrency, and transactional outbox evidence; reject a replay before any external effect; then make OpenAPI and runtime disagree on nullability and block the release. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Persist state and an outbox fact atomically, publish with deduplication and ordering assumptions, and reconcile stuck or duplicate delivery.

For Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Lab Outbox Consistency, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: Publishing an event immediately after COMMIT makes state and message delivery atomic.
